page: Amazon Web Services c8id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a resource ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 138344.04 and a PassMark CPU score of 123092.36. Memory performance is highly competitive, achieving top-tier status in both cached and uncached read-only memory bandwidth tests, alongside a strong PassMark memory score. Single-core performance, however, sits in the average tier, indicating that the instance's primary strength lies in parallelized, multi-threaded execution.

The processor includes advanced vector and matrix extensions, such as AVX-512 and AMX, which drive top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and medium-model text generation. The presence of 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, combined with top-tier Redis SET operations and database performance, makes this instance highly cost-efficient for data-intensive, high-throughput workloads. It offers a dense allocation of compute and fast local storage without the overhead of external network storage. Appropriate workloads include high-performance database hosting, software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, and local LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a bundled 7600 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though single-core metrics remain in the average tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, enabling top-tier results in database operations, software compilation, and LLM inference prompt processing. This hardware profile provides high qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized, storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for multi-threaded data processing, high-throughput databases, and local machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance compute and database workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute instance featuring massive local NVMe storage and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 7600 GB of bundled NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AMX for accelerated vector and matrix operations
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for high-throughput databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing
